attributes.name: Description
attributes.content: The UCLA Library Digital Collections includes rare and unique digital materials developed by the UCLA Library to support education, research, service, and creative expression. This website is our new interface for discovery and engagement of these collections. See collections of historic photographs and manuscripts. More collections added weekly.

attributes.class: blacklight-work document document__list-item blacklight-work document-position-0 document-position-11

childNodes.textContent: Montage photograph of Barbara Lee Tramutto in 5 poses in ethnic costumes, Santa Monica, 1951

Composite of 5 photographs, all of Barbara Lee Tramuto posing or dancing in Spanish-style, Indian-style, Polynesian-style, and beaded and caped costumes, indoors with wall in background. The photographs were taken at the home of Adelbert Bartlett, located at 535 15th St., Santa Monica.

attributes.class: blacklight-work document document__list-item blacklight-work document-position-0 document-position-12

childNodes.textContent: Group of Mojave Indians mourning over the body of Chief Sistuma, Arizona, circa 1902
Original photograph by C.C. (Charles C.) Pierce. Caption on the photo notes the copyright date as 1902 by C.C. Pierce & Co. (photo numbered “1402”). Copy print date unknown.

attributes.class: blacklight-work document document__list-item blacklight-work document-position-0 document-position-13

childNodes.textContent: Indigenous Africans conversing with a missionary, Central Africa, 1928-1948

Copy photograph of villagers from an unidentified Central African nation, with Anglo missionaries. Men, women and children are represented in the photo; men and boys are seated on the ground with the missionaries, while the women and girls stand. The women wear wrappers (textiles wrapped around their…

childNodes.textContent: Portrait of Mrs. Oscar Rittmaster, Santa Monica, 1956
Portrait of Mrs. Oscar (Maude) Rittmaster, seated with arm resting on chair, wearing a skirt and blouse with broach. Photographed at Adelbert Bartlett’s home (535 15th Street).

attributes.class: blacklight-work document document__list-item blacklight-work document-position-0 document-position-15

childNodes.textContent: Portrait of Mrs. Oscar Rittmaster, Santa Monica, 1956
Anne Maude Rittmaster (noted as Maude Rittmaster or Mrs. Oscar Rittmaster) was a member of the Santa Monica Civic Opera Association Board of Directors.

childNodes.textContent: Group of People on Navy Day, Santa Monica, 1934

This image is of a group of people, likely standing on the ship the U.S. Philip. The two men in the picture are dressed in naval uniforms, while the women wear fashionable day dresses and hats. The woman on the far left has her arm raised, her hand resting on part of the ship. Behind them, the ocean…
